title: Rivian Debt Structure and Capital Refinancing
text: Rivian Automotive currently shows a Debt to Equity Ratio of 1.49, suggesting moderate leverage with a balanced mix of debt and equity financing. Different sources report varying ratios; as of late 2025, Rivian's D/E ratio was around 0.98, meaning the company had nearly as much debt as shareholder equity on its balance sheet. The company's Debt to Assets Ratio stands at 0.46 and Debt to Capital at 0.60. Rivian Automotive reported $6.65B in Debt for its fiscal quarter ending in December 2025. RIVN's debt to equity ratio has increased from 1.9% to 96.6% over the past 5 years. Cantor Fitzgerald maintained its Overweight rating following Rivian's recent debt refinancing announcement, with proceeds planned to redeem the company's existing $1.25 billion floating rate senior secured notes due in 2026. Rivian is considering a high-yield bond sale led by JPMorgan Chase to potentially raise up to $2 billion for refinancing its debt maturing in 2026. Rivian Automotive displays poor financial strength, usually caused by too much debt for the company. However, their liquidity position is strong for the near-term, backed by a significant cash reserve and healthy current ratios.
